name: Nature / Nature-family Journals
notes: Nature format: Results BEFORE Methods. Short abstract (150 words). Main text ~3000-5000 words. Methods section at end. Double-spaced. Numbered references. Up to 8 display items (figures + tables combined). Extended Data figures allowed. Online Methods section for detailed methodology.
writing_guide:
TONE: Accessible to scientists across all disciplines. Avoid jargon; define every technical term on first use. Active voice strongly preferred ('We found...', 'These results demonstrate...'). Write with narrative clarity; each paragraph should tell part of a story. Aim for a non-specialist reader to understand your key findings.

ABSTRACT: Maximum 150 words. Single paragraph. Structure: 1-2 sentences of accessible context, 1 sentence stating the gap, 2-3 sentences on approach and key findings, 1 sentence on significance/implications. No references, no abbreviations.

STRUCTURE: Results BEFORE Methods. This is critical. Introduction should be 2-3 paragraphs (no subheadings), ending with a single sentence stating what the paper shows. Results section carries the narrative: organize by findings, not by experiments. Discussion is separate from Results. Methods go at the end. Main text must be 3000-5000 words total.

CITATIONS: Use superscript numbered references. 30-50 total. Keep citations minimal in text; Nature values concision. Do not use citation clusters of more than 3-4 refs.

FIGURES/TABLES: Maximum 8 display items (figures + tables combined). Each must be essential. Use multi-panel figures (a, b, c, d) to maximize information density. Extended Data figures for supplementary items. Figures carry much of the story; design them to be understood independently.

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Broad significance beyond your subfield. Strong effect sizes. Thorough controls. Data availability statement.

DO NOT: Use subheadings in Introduction. Do not put Methods before Results. Do not use field-specific jargon without definition. Do not exceed 8 display items. Do not write more than 5000 words in main text.
